verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex (latent TGFB1 bound to LAP), but in specific cell types (regulatory T cells, myeloid cells) the latent complex is stably anchored to the cell surface via membrane proteins GARP and LRRC33. The TGFB1 protein body itself has no membrane anchor, but the latent TGFB1/LAP complex is held at the surface through non-covalent, wash-resistant association with these TM partners. Integrins then engage and activate the surface-bound latent complex. This surface presentation is well-documented and context-dependent (tissue/cell-type restricted to GARP/LRRC33-expressing cells), making it contextual surface accessibility via stable surface attachment to a TM partner.
